summary: The rawrr package provides access to proprietary Thermo Fisher Scientific Orbitrap instrument data as a stand-alone R package or serves as a backend for the Bioconductor Spectra package.
The example file 20181113_010_autoQC01.raw contains Fourier-transformed Orbitrap spectra recorded on a Thermo Fisher Scientific Q Exactive HF mass spectrometer in positive mode.
MS2 spectra in the example file were generated by HCD fragmentation at normalized collision energy of 27.
The analyzed sample consisted of the iRT peptide mix in a tryptic BSA digest separated using a 20 min linear gradient on C18 reversed-phase material.
The readSpectrum function returns a rawrrSpectrum object or rawrrSpectrumSet.
Scan 9594 was acquired using a transient of 64 ms, equal to a resolving power of 30,000 at 200 m/z.
Scan 9594 was acquired with an AGC target of 1e5 elementary charges.
The API provides 119 data items for the scan shown in the example, covering ion optics settings, mass calibration, and other diagnostic data.
For the scan example, the C-trap collected 100,000 charges within 2.8 milliseconds.
Orbitrap detectors follow the relationship S/N ∝ charges · √R.
The example LC-MS run was recorded by parallel reaction monitoring (PRM) with the parent ion 487.2567 isolated in regularly-spaced intervals.
The delta between consecutive scans in the PRM example is always 22 scans (one PRM cycle).
All spectra in the example file were written after applying centroiding and lock mass correction.
Centroided data allows graphing spectra using signal-to-noise as response value.
